description: Headless, accessible product tour library for React. Ships tours, hints, onboarding checklists, product announcements, adoption tracking, analytics, media embeds, scheduling, and microsurveys. WCAG 2.1 AA compliant, TypeScript strict, tree-shakeable. Three MIT-licensed core packages (@tour-kit/core, @tour-kit/react, @tour-kit/hints) plus an optional Pro suite.
rules: Core logic lives in @tour-kit/core as headless hooks and utilities. UI components live in @tour-kit/react and are thin wrappers over core hooks — never duplicate logic between packages.

Compound components use the Unified Slot pattern for `asChild` composition. Both Radix-style (element cloning via `children={<MyComponent />}`) and Base UI-style (render prop via `children={(props) => <MyComponent {...props} />}`) are supported — prefer render prop when you need access to the computed props.

All interactive components are WCAG 2.1 AA compliant with focus management, keyboard navigation, ARIA live regions, and reduced-motion support built in. Do not disable these defaults without explicit accessibility review.
TypeScript is strict mode with `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`. Never use `any`; prefer `unknown` + narrowing. Component prop interfaces are exported from the package root with the component.
Bundle budgets are enforced: @tour-kit/core < 8KB gzipped, @tour-kit/react < 12KB, @tour-kit/hints < 5KB. When adding features, measure with size-limit or bundlephobia before merging.

Free packages (MIT): core, react, hints. Pro packages (commercial license, $99 lifetime): adoption, analytics, announcements, checklists, media, scheduling, surveys. Do not import Pro packages in examples targeting free-tier users.
Router integration for multi-page tours uses adapters — `createNextAppRouterAdapter`, `createNextPagesRouterAdapter`, `createReactRouterAdapter`. Never hardcode route awareness into tour components.
Persistence uses storage adapters. Default is localStorage via `createLocalStorageAdapter`; SSR-safe builds should use `createMemoryStorageAdapter` on the server side.

Providers compose: TourKitProvider wraps multi-tour apps; TourProvider scopes a single tour. Extended packages add their own providers (AdoptionProvider, ChecklistProvider, etc.) inside TourKitProvider.
For integration with shadcn/ui, Radix, or Base UI, use the `asChild` prop on TourCard, TourNavigation, TourClose, and related compound parts — do not override the internal primitives directly.
